title: Nutty Flaky Polish Almonds Crumble
description: A savory and nutty dish perfect for a solo dinner featuring flavors of cooking wine and alderwood smoke, served as a side or light meal.
instructions: Mix almonds with almond meal and a dash of salt. Set aside.
Dice onion and mince garlic, then sauté in butter until translucent.
Add pork loin to the pan along with cooking wine, cover, and bake for about 25 minutes or until internal temperature reaches 145°F (medium-rare).
Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Season with salt and pepper.
Crush cooled almond mixture in a blender or food processor until it forms small crumb-like pieces.
Spread the crushed almond mixture evenly on top of cooled meat, then bake uncovered for about 10 minutes or until golden brown and crunchy.
Serve warm as a side dish with a side of steamed vegetables.
